name: Portugal
taxType: Flat rate on eligible foreign income
requirements: Proof of remote employment or freelance income
Minimum income proof (4x Portuguese minimum wage)
intro: Portugal's ITS regime replaced the NHR in 2024 and remains one of Europe's most attractive tax frameworks for digital nomads. The 20% flat rate on foreign income applies for a full decade, and the country's mild climate, affordable coast, and Schengen access make it a top-tier destination.

applicationSteps.title: Check Eligibility
applicationSteps.desc: Ensure your monthly income exceeds €3,280 and you have no criminal record in the last 5 years.

applicationSteps.title: Secure Health Insurance
applicationSteps.desc: Obtain a policy with global coverage. SafetyWing's Nomad Insurance is the standard for 2026 applications.

applicationSteps.title: Prepare Documents
applicationSteps.desc: Translate and apostille your remote work contract, bank statements, and criminal record check.

applicationSteps.title: Apply at Consulate
applicationSteps.desc: Submit your application at the nearest Portuguese consulate or via the official online portal.

applicationSteps.title: Register NIF & Address
applicationSteps.desc: Upon arrival, register for your Portuguese tax number (NIF) and secure a local lease agreement.

documentChecklist: Proof of Remote Work (Contract or Freelance Agreement)
guide.taxDeepDive: The ITS regime replaced NHR in 2024. For digital nomads, it offers a 20% flat tax on eligible foreign-sourced income for 10 years. To qualify, you must not have been a tax resident of Portugal in the previous 5 years.

faq.a: Yes, dependents can apply alongside you, though additional income requirements apply for each family member.

faq.q: Do I need to stay in Portugal full-time?
faq.a: To maintain residency and tax benefits, you should spend 183 days per year in the country.

name: Spain
taxType: Flat rate on income up to €600,000
requirements: Remote employment contract or freelance proof
No Spanish tax residency in prior 5 years
intro: Spain's Beckham Law offers a 24% flat tax for up to 6 years on income up to €600,000. The Digital Nomad Visa launched in 2023 has streamlined the process, making cities like Valencia and Madrid among Europe's most popular nomad hubs.

applicationSteps.title: Verify Income
applicationSteps.desc: Ensure your monthly earnings exceed €3,105 (200% of Spanish SMI for 2026).

applicationSteps.title: Apply for NIE
applicationSteps.desc: Secure your Spanish Foreigner Identification Number from a consulate or local police station.

applicationSteps.title: Private Health Insurance
applicationSteps.desc: Obtain a comprehensive Spanish policy with no co-payments or waiting periods.

applicationSteps.title: Online Submission
applicationSteps.desc: Submit your application via the UGE portal for an initial 3-year residency permit.

applicationSteps.title: Tax Registration
applicationSteps.desc: Apply for the Beckham Law special tax regime within 6 months of arriving in Spain.

guide.taxDeepDive: The Beckham Law allows nomads to pay a flat 24% tax for 6 years. Crucially, foreign-sourced investment income and capital gains are often exempt, making it a premier choice for high-net-worth nomads.

faq.q: Can I work for Spanish companies?
faq.a: Yes, as long as Spanish income accounts for less than 20% of your total revenue.
The SMI is updated annually; for 2026, the required income threshold is €3,105/month for a single applicant.

name: UAE
intro: The UAE's 0% personal income tax is the gold standard for high-earning nomads. Dubai's Virtual Working Visa requires $5,000/month income but delivers unmatched infrastructure, safety, and global flight connectivity.

applicationSteps.title: Check Income
applicationSteps.desc: Minimum monthly income of $5,000 for the last 3 months.

applicationSteps.title: Obtain UAE Insurance
applicationSteps.desc: Health insurance with UAE coverage is mandatory (SafetyWing's global policy is accepted).

applicationSteps.title: Online Application
applicationSteps.desc: Apply via the GDRFA or ICP portal for the 1-year Virtual Working Visa.

applicationSteps.title: Medical Test
applicationSteps.desc: Once in Dubai, complete a simple medical screening (blood test & X-ray).

applicationSteps.title: Emirates ID
applicationSteps.desc: Biometrics appointment and issuance of your physical Emirates ID card.

guide.taxDeepDive: The UAE has 0% personal income tax. There is no tax filing required for individuals on the Virtual Working Visa. This makes Dubai the most financially efficient city in the world for high-earning remote workers.

faq.q: Do I need a local sponsor?
faq.a: No, the Virtual Working Visa is self-sponsored.

faq.q: Can I open a local bank account?
faq.a: Yes, once you have your Emirates ID, you can open accounts with major banks like ENBD or Mashreq.

name: Thailand
taxType: Flat rate on local income for work permit holders
requirements: Proof of income $80,000/year or $40,000 with qualifications
intro: Thailand's LTR Visa is Asia's best long-term residency deal in 2026. The 10-year duration with 15% flat tax combines with one of the world's lowest cost-of-living indexes to create exceptional savings potential.

applicationSteps.title: Verify LTR Category
applicationSteps.desc: Choose 'Remote Professional' or 'Wealthy Individual' based on your income.

applicationSteps.title: Document Collection
applicationSteps.desc: Gather 2 years of tax returns and proof of company revenue ($150M+ or listed).

applicationSteps.title: Apply via BOI
applicationSteps.desc: Submit your application to the Board of Investment (BOI) online portal.

applicationSteps.title: Endorsement Letter
applicationSteps.desc: Receive your qualification endorsement letter within 20 working days.

applicationSteps.title: Visa Issuance
applicationSteps.desc: Pick up your 10-year visa at any Thai embassy or at the OSOS center in Bangkok.

documentChecklist: Proof of $80k/year income (or $40k with Master's degree)
Employment contract with a qualified company
Company financial reports (min $150M revenue last 3 years)
Tax returns for the last 2 years
guide.taxDeepDive: LTR visa holders benefit from a 15% flat tax on Thai-sourced income. Foreign-sourced income not remitted to Thailand in the same tax year is generally tax-free under current interpretation.

faq.a: No, LTR holders are exempt from the standard 90-day reporting and instead report annually.

faq.q: Does the visa include a work permit?
faq.a: Yes, a digital work permit is included, allowing you to work for your foreign employer legally.

name: Indonesia
intro: Indonesia's B211A visa charges 0% tax on foreign-sourced income, making Bali the world's most popular tropical nomad base. The $2,000/month income requirement is among the lowest globally.

name: Japan
taxType: No local tax for visa duration
intro: Japan launched its Digital Nomad Visa in 2024 allowing 6-month stays with no local tax obligation. The high income requirement ($5,330/month) filters for professionals who can fully enjoy Tokyo's world-class safety and infrastructure.

name: Georgia
intro: Georgia's 1% small business tax regime is the lowest in Europe. The 'Remotely from Georgia' program grants 1-year residency to remote workers earning at least $2,000/month, with visa-free entry for 95+ nationalities.

applicationSteps.title: Small Business Registry
applicationSteps.desc: Apply for 'Small Business Status' to lock in the 1% tax rate.

applicationSteps.title: Open Local Bank Account
applicationSteps.desc: Open an account at TBC or Bank of Georgia for business operations.

applicationSteps.title: Monthly Filings
applicationSteps.desc: Set up a simple accounting process for 1% monthly revenue payments.

applicationSteps.title: Health Insurance
applicationSteps.desc: Maintain local or global health insurance (SafetyWing is recommended).

applicationSteps.title: Residency Card
applicationSteps.desc: Optionally apply for a physical residency card after 6 months of stay.

documentChecklist: Georgia address proof (utility bill or lease)
guide.taxDeepDive: Georgia offers a 1% tax rate on turnover for registered small businesses (income up to ~€170,000/year). It is the lowest legal tax rate for freelancers in the EMEA region.

faq.q: Is the 1% tax on profit or revenue?
faq.a: The 1% is on total revenue (turnover), which simplifies accounting significantly.

faq.q: Can I stay for 1 year visa-free?
faq.a: Yes, 95+ countries get a 365-day visa-free entry, making it the most flexible nomad destination.

name: Greece
taxType: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 7 years
intro: Greece offers the EU's lowest flat tax for foreign income earners at just 7% for 7 years. Combined with Schengen access and Mediterranean island-hopping, it's one of Europe's most compelling nomad packages.

name: Colombia
intro: Colombia's territorial tax system means foreign-sourced income is completely exempt from local tax. The Digital Nomad Visa requires just $900/month income — the lowest threshold of any formal nomad visa program globally.

name: Mexico
taxType: Progressive rates, avg ~25% for nomad income levels
intro: Mexico's Temporary Resident Visa doesn't specifically target nomads, but its accessibility and US time zone alignment make it Latin America's most popular nomad corridor. Tax residency rules are complex — most nomads stay under 183 days to avoid local obligations.

name: Croatia
taxType: Exempt from local tax on foreign income
requirements: Proof of remote work for foreign employer
intro: Croatia's Digital Nomad Visa fully exempts foreign-sourced income from local taxation. As an EU and Schengen member with Adriatic coast access, it's one of the most appealing 0% tax options in Europe.

name: Estonia
intro: Estonia pioneered the e-Residency concept and continues to lead in digital governance. The 20% corporate tax is deferred until profits are distributed, effectively allowing tax-free reinvestment for growing businesses.

name: Lithuania
intro: Lithuania's fintech-friendly environment and 15% flat tax for freelancers make Vilnius an increasingly attractive Baltic alternative to Tallinn. Living costs are 15-20% lower than Estonia.

name: Hungary
intro: Hungary's White Card program and 15% flat tax make Budapest the EU's most affordable capital for digital nomads. Monthly rents under $1,000 in the city center are nearly unheard of elsewhere in the European Union.

name: Czech Republic
taxType: 15% flat tax on trade license income
intro: The Czech Republic's trade license (Živnostenský List) gives freelancers a clear path to residency with a 15% flat tax. Prague's central European location makes it an ideal base for nomads who travel frequently within the EU.

name: South Korea
taxType: Flat rate for workcation visa holders
intro: South Korea's Workcation Visa targets tech professionals with 5G infrastructure and the world's fastest average internet speeds. Seoul's café culture and cutting-edge urban design create a unique nomad experience.

name: Singapore
taxType: No tax on foreign-sourced income not remitted
intro: Singapore's Tech.Pass targets high-earning tech professionals with effectively 0% tax on foreign income not remitted locally. The city-state is Asia's premier business hub with English as a primary language.

name: Vietnam
taxType: Low effective rate for short-stay workers
intro: Vietnam's e-visa system allows 90-day stays with easy renewals. While there's no formal nomad visa, the ultra-low cost of living and booming economy make Ho Chi Minh City and Da Nang popular with budget-focused remote workers.

name: Malaysia
intro: Malaysia's DE Rantau pass is Southeast Asia's most underrated nomad visa in 2026. Zero tax on foreign income, a low $2,000/month requirement, and widespread English make Kuala Lumpur an exceptional value proposition.

name: Armenia
intro: Armenia offers visa-free entry for 180 days to citizens of 80+ countries. The 10% flat tax for individual entrepreneurs and Yerevan's ultra-low rents make it the Caucasus' most accessible nomad base.

name: Turkey
intro: Turkey lacks a formal digital nomad visa but its tourist visa (90/180 days) and affordable residence permits attract many nomads. Istanbul's east-meets-west culture and dramatically low costs for luxury living make it a compelling short-term base.

name: Canada
intro: Canada's Working Holiday Visa gives young professionals (18-35) 1-2 years of legal work authorization. While taxes are higher than nomad-specific visas, Canada's tech sector and quality of life attract career-focused remote workers.

name: Argentina
taxType: Non-residents not taxed on foreign income
intro: Argentina's favorable blue-dollar exchange rate makes it extraordinarily cheap for USD-earning nomads. Non-residents pay 0% tax on foreign income, and Buenos Aires offers a European lifestyle at South American prices.

name: Brazil
intro: Brazil's Digital Nomad Visa requires just $1,500/month income — one of the lowest in the Americas. The visa exempts holders from local tax obligations during the first year, making Rio and São Paulo accessible to mid-income remote workers.

name: Costa Rica
intro: Costa Rica's territorial tax system means foreign income is completely exempt. The Digital Nomad Visa requires $3,000/month income and grants access to both Pacific and Caribbean coasts from a single, stable Central American base.

name: Panama
intro: Panama is one of few countries using the US Dollar as official currency, eliminating all exchange rate risk. The territorial tax system exempts foreign income entirely, making it a natural fit for US-based nomads.

name: South Africa
intro: South Africa's Remote Working Visa and the rand's weakness against major currencies create exceptional purchasing power. Cape Town's natural beauty and growing tech hub make it Africa's most popular nomad city by far.

name: Kenya
intro: Kenya's 'Silicon Savannah' is Africa's leading tech ecosystem. Nairobi's digital nomad work permit gives access to a rapidly growing economy with English as an official language and world-class safari experiences on weekends.

name: Romania
intro: Romania boasts the EU's lowest flat tax at 10% combined with some of the fastest internet speeds in the world. Bucharest's cost of living is among the lowest of any EU capital, maximizing take-home savings.

name: Germany
taxType: Progressive, avg ~22% for typical nomad income
requirements: Proof of freelance work and clients
intro: Germany's Freiberufler (freelance) visa is one of Europe's most established paths for independent workers. Berlin's tech scene and relatively affordable rents (by Western European standards) make it the top pick for EU-based freelancers.

name: Netherlands
taxType: Progressive, avg ~30% for typical nomad income
intro: The Dutch-American Friendship Treaty (DAFT) makes the Netherlands uniquely accessible for US entrepreneurs with just a €4,500 deposit. Amsterdam's English-speaking environment and dense startup ecosystem offset higher tax rates.

name: Philippines
intro: The Philippines' English-speaking workforce and ultra-low costs make it accessible for budget nomads. While lacking a formal nomad visa, the tourist visa can be extended for up to 3 years, giving long-term flexibility.

name: Bulgaria
intro: Bulgaria offers the EU's lowest flat tax at just 10% and some of the most affordable living costs in the Union. Bansko has become a world-famous nomad hub for its winter sports and low taxes, while Sofia offers a fast-growing tech scene and urban amenities.

name: Serbia
intro: Serbia has streamlined its residence permits for remote workers and entrepreneurs, offering up to 3 years of stability. Belgrade is a rising star in the Balkans, known for its incredible nightlife, cafe culture, and low cost of living compared to Western Europe.

name: Albania
taxType: 0% tax on IT/remote income for non-residents
intro: Albania is Europe's latest nomad frontier, offering a 'Unique Permit' for remote workers and 0% tax on IT-related income. Tirana is a colorful, affordable capital with a Mediterranean climate and easy access to the spectacular Albanian Riviera.

name: Uruguay
highlights: Highest quality of life in LatAm
intro: Uruguay is Latin America's most stable and progressive democracy, offering a territorial tax system where foreign income is not taxed. The Digital Nomad Permit is exceptionally easy to get, making Montevideo a premium, stress-free base.

name: Guatemala
taxType: Low effective rate for foreign professionals
intro: Guatemala's new 'Remote Professional' permit launched in late 2025, offering up to 5 years of residency. Antigua is the crown jewel of Central America, providing a stunning colonial setting and vibrant nomad community with easy US time zone alignment.

name: Slovakia
intro: Slovakia offers a stable, affordable Central European base with a straightforward 15% flat tax for small businesses. Bratislava is just an hour from Vienna but offers a much lower cost of living and a growing community of tech professionals.

name: Peru
intro: Peru is a global cultural and culinary leader. While a formal nomad visa is in the works, most remote workers use the generous 183-day tourist stay. Cusco offers an unmatched blend of Incan history and affordable mountain living for under $1,200/month.
